radios.name: Baofeng UV-5R
radios.description: Popular budget dual-band handheld that is widely used by beginners.

sections.heading: Why operators start here
sections.body: Low entry cost and broad community usage make this one of the most common first handheld radios.

sections.heading: What to expect
sections.body: Great value for local FM use, though you should expect a basic interface and simple build quality.

radios.name: Yaesu FT-65
radios.description: Robust entry-level handheld from a well-known manufacturer.

sections.heading: Who this is for
sections.body: Ideal for new licence holders who want dependable operation and better ergonomics than ultra-budget models.

sections.heading: Field notes
sections.body: Clear audio and straightforward controls make this a practical daily radio for repeaters and simplex.

radios.name: Anytone AT-D878UVII Plus
radios.description: Feature-rich handheld for operators wanting both analogue and DMR.

sections.heading: Why it stands out
sections.body: Strong DMR capability and extensive configuration make it a versatile step-up for experienced users.

sections.heading: Before buying
sections.body: Expect a deeper setup process and codeplug work compared with entry-level analog-only radios.
